methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $10M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
General Galactic, a seed-stage climate and energy company founded by Halen Mattison and Luke Neise, has raised $10M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 9 private and 1 public company in the Climate & Energy sector at similar or adjacent stages, including peers such as TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, and Commonwealth Fusion Systems.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the $10M total raised as a proxy base value and applying a 2.5x multiplier typical for seed-to-next-stage advancement, implies a valuation of $25M. Peer-median analysis across three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables—Graphyte ($200M, Series A), Fourth Power ($150M, Series A), and Steady Energy ($110M, Series A)—yields a median implied valuation of $150M, with a range of $110M to $200M.

Triangulation of these two methods produces a low estimate of $21M, a median estimate of $88M, and a high estimate of $172M. The wide spread reflects material stage-based discounting between the peer set (predominantly Series A) and General Galactic's seed position, as well as inherent uncertainty in applying peer multiples to a pre-revenue company. Key sensitivities include the timing of the next funding round, actual revenue trajectory relative to peers, and sector-specific risk premiums. Additional operating metrics would refine these bounds.
